Package com.audiodo.aspen

Class ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.audiodo.aspen.ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks
  • public abstract class ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks
extends java.lang.Object

      abstract void aborted​(CalibrationCapabilityAbortReason reason)
      Signals that the calibration has been aborted.

      Signals that the calibration has been aborted by the audio device.
      abstract void calibrationComplete()
      Signals that the calibration is complete

      Signals that the calibration is complete.
      abstract void calibrationDowntime()
      Signals that the calibration is busy tearing down the previous calibration.
      abstract void channelComplete()
      Signals that a channel is complete

      Signals that a channel is now complete.
      void delete()  
      protected void finalize()  
      abstract void frequencyComplete()
      Signals that a frequency is complete

      Signals that a frequency is now complete.
      protected static long getCPtr​(ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks obj)  
      abstract void interruptedPeerDisconnected()
      Signals that the calibration has been interrupted due to a peer disconnect.

      Signals that the calibration has been interrupted as the peer earbud has disconnected.
      Reconnect the peer to be able to resume calibration.
      abstract void peerConnected()
      Signals that the peer has been connected.
      protected void swigDirectorDisconnect()  
      protected static long swigRelease​(ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks obj)  
      void swigReleaseOwnership()  
      void swigTakeOwnership()  
      abstract void toneNotPlaying()
      Signals that a tone has stopped playing

      Signals that a tone has stopped playing in the audio device.
      This should stop any animation or similar.
      abstract void tonePlaying​(CalibrationCapabilityProgressData progress)
      Signals that a tone is playing and contains progress data

      Signals that a tone is playing in the audio device.

      • delete

        public void delete()

      • swigDirectorDisconnect

        protected void swigDirectorDisconnect()

      • swigReleaseOwnership

        public void swigReleaseOwnership()

      • swigTakeOwnership

        public void swigTakeOwnership()

      • tonePlaying

        public abstract void tonePlaying​(CalibrationCapabilityProgressData progress)
        Signals that a tone is playing and contains progress data

        Signals that a tone is playing in the audio device. It also contains
        data of the progress of the calibration.

      • toneNotPlaying

        public abstract void toneNotPlaying()
        Signals that a tone has stopped playing

        Signals that a tone has stopped playing in the audio device.
        This should stop any animation or similar.

      • frequencyComplete

        public abstract void frequencyComplete()
        Signals that a frequency is complete

        Signals that a frequency is now complete. Any animation to
        inform user that the calibration will change to a new frequency
        should be done now.

      • channelComplete

        public abstract void channelComplete()
        Signals that a channel is complete

        Signals that a channel is now complete. Any animation to inform
        user that the calibration will continue on the other ear should be
        done now.

      • calibrationComplete

        public abstract void calibrationComplete()
        Signals that the calibration is complete

        Signals that the calibration is complete. A result is now available
        by calling get_result().

      • aborted

        public abstract void aborted​(CalibrationCapabilityAbortReason reason)
        Signals that the calibration has been aborted.

        Signals that the calibration has been aborted by the audio device. One
        cause could be an incoming call. The calibration will have to be restarted
        when the interruption has passed.

        Parameters:
        reason - The reason why the calibration was aborted

      • interruptedPeerDisconnected

        public abstract void interruptedPeerDisconnected()
        Signals that the calibration has been interrupted due to a peer disconnect.

        Signals that the calibration has been interrupted as the peer earbud has disconnected.
        Reconnect the peer to be able to resume calibration.

      • peerConnected

        public abstract void peerConnected()
        Signals that the peer has been connected.

      • calibrationDowntime

        public abstract void calibrationDowntime()
        Signals that the calibration is busy tearing down the previous calibration.
